reasoning: There are currently no approved human vaccines for Hendra virus. A Phase I trial for a candidate human vaccine reported results in mid-2024, but it takes several years to progress from Phase I to regulatory approval, making licensure by November 2025 highly unlikely. An equine vaccine (Equivac HeV) has been available since 2012, yet it has not been listed on the specific Wikipedia 'List of infectious diseases' page in the 13 years since its release. Given that the page focuses generally on human diseases, it is unlikely the horse vaccine will be suddenly added. Furthermore, Wikipedia edits on this specific list are infrequent. Barring an unprecedentedly fast emergency approval of a human vaccine or anomalous Wikipedia editing behavior that circumvents moderation, the status will remain unlisted (i.e., 'No'). Given the cumulative chance of an unexpected Wikipedia edit or classification criteria parsing error over time, probabilities rise slightly across the horizons but remain close to the floor.
